title: • Reflection: What did I try to "let go" of today? Did it bring
content:
• Reflection: What did I try to "let go" of today? Did it bring peace or just a different kind of struggle?

    • Answer: Today I tried being myself more and doing things differently. I tried letting go of being someone who needs another person to be able to do the simple task of breathing. I tried letting go of a toxic cycle. Even if negative emotions did come about, I continued to breathe good energy in and out, even if it meant I needed to isolate myself for some time to recollect my thought and get back on the path. What I found in that empty space can be best described as a feeling of being okay with myself, something closer to the opposite of self loathing, maybe just a little glimpse of whatever that may be considered. I did not constantly dread being myself today. Tomorrow I hope to gain even further strength and stamina.


    Day 4
    MORNING
    • Morning Reading: "What is meditation? What is leaving one’s body? What is fasting? What is holding one’s breath? It is fleeing from the Self, it is a brief escape from the torment of being Self, it is a temporary palliative against the pain and folly of life."
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 10 (chunk 10/29)
